reason: Modifying the /etc/fstab entry for /home changes the filesystem mount options, which can affect the ability to boot or mount the home partition correctly. If the entry is malformed or the system does not support the nosuid option for that filesystem, it could prevent user logins or cause boot failures. Policy classifies partitioning/filesystem changes as too risky for automated remediation.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; potential boot or mount failure; possible loss of user login capability; setuid-dependent applications may break
